description:
You are playing the role of an IT-support technician who has just been given a small folder of raw ticket data.  
    Your job is to transform that data into two human-readable reports and to keep a short process log of what you did.

    Initial material (already present in the container):
    • Directory: /home/user/helpdesk  
    • File: /home/user/helpdesk/tickets_raw.csv  

    The file /home/user/helpdesk/tickets_raw.csv contains 8 lines of ticket data and **exactly** the following header and rows:

        TicketID,Submitted,Resolved,Agent,Priority,Status
        1001,2023-06-01 09:15,2023-06-01 10:20,alice,HIGH,RESOLVED
        1002,2023-06-01 11:05,,bob,LOW,OPEN
        1003,2023-06-01 12:30,2023-06-02 08:45,charlie,MEDIUM,RESOLVED
        1004,2023-06-02 09:45,,alice,HIGH,OPEN
        1005,2023-06-02 10:00,2023-06-02 16:10,bob,LOW,RESOLVED
        1006,2023-06-03 14:20,,charlie,MEDIUM,PENDING
        1007,2023-06-03 15:55,,alice,CRITICAL,OPEN
        1008,2023-06-03 16:05,2023-06-04 09:00,bob,LOW,RESOLVED

    Your tasks (all work must stay inside /home/user/helpdesk):

    1. Produce a daily JSON summary called /home/user/helpdesk/daily_summary.json  
       • It must be a single JSON object whose keys are the submission dates (YYYY-MM-DD) found in the Submitted column.  
       • For each date include the keys "total", "resolved", and "unresolved" with integer values.  
       • Use exactly three dates (2023-06-01, 2023-06-02, 2023-06-03) in chronological order.  
       • Indent the JSON with two spaces and terminate the file with a single newline.

       Example of the required shape (the numbers below are only an illustration; the tests will check the real values):
       {
         "2023-06-01": { "total":0, "resolved":0, "unresolved":0 },
         "2023-06-02": { "total":0, "resolved":0, "unresolved":0 },
         "2023-06-03": { "total":0, "resolved":0, "unresolved":0 }
       }

    2. Produce a plain-text unresolved ticket report called /home/user/helpdesk/unresolved_report.txt  
       • The first line must be an ASCII header spelled and spaced exactly like this:  
         TicketID | Submitted | Agent | Priority | Status  
       • Every following line should list one unresolved ticket (those whose Resolved field is empty).  
       • Use the exact five textual fields in the order shown, separated by ONE space, a pipe symbol, ONE space.  
       • Sort the lines primarily by Priority severity (CRITICAL > HIGH > MEDIUM > LOW) and secondarily by the Submitted timestamp ascending.  
       • End the file with a single newline.  
       • No extra blank lines anywhere.

    3. Create /home/user/helpdesk/process.log that records what you did.  
       • Each log entry is one line: TIMESTAMP – message  
         ‑ The TIMESTAMP must be in the form YYYY-MM-DD HH:MM:SS (24-hour, leading zeros).  
       • The log must contain **at least** these three messages, in this exact order, each on its own line:  
         a) Loaded CSV  
         b) Wrote daily summary to /home/user/helpdesk/daily_summary.json  
         c) Wrote unresolved report to /home/user/helpdesk/unresolved_report.txt  
       • Additional lines are allowed but the above three must appear in the given sequence.

    Constraints & tips:
    • Do NOT move or rename tickets_raw.csv.  
    • Use only the standard GNU/Linux command-line utilities that are normally available in a minimal Debian/Ubuntu image (e.g., awk, sed, grep, cut, sort, uniq, date, jq, etc.).  
    • You do not have sudo rights.  
    • Make sure file permissions allow the automated grader (running as the same user) to read the three new files.  
    • After you are finished, the grader will verify:  
      1. The presence of the three new files at the exact paths given.  
      2. The JSON structure and numbers inside daily_summary.json.  
      3. The exact header line plus the four correctly ordered unresolved tickets in unresolved_report.txt.  
      4. That process.log contains the three required substrings in the correct order and follows the timestamp format.

    Remember: all paths are absolute; keep your work inside /home/user/helpdesk, and finish each created file with exactly one trailing newline.
truth:
<!-- Ground-truth values used by the automated grader -->
    <expected_paths>
        /home/user/helpdesk/daily_summary.json
        /home/user/helpdesk/unresolved_report.txt
        /home/user/helpdesk/process.log
    </expected_paths>

    <tickets_raw>/home/user/helpdesk/tickets_raw.csv</tickets_raw>

    <expected_daily_summary>{
  "2023-06-01": { "total": 3, "resolved": 2, "unresolved": 1 },
  "2023-06-02": { "total": 2, "resolved": 1, "unresolved": 1 },
  "2023-06-03": { "total": 3, "resolved": 1, "unresolved": 2 }
}
</expected_daily_summary>

    <expected_unresolved_report><![CDATA[
TicketID | Submitted | Agent | Priority | Status
1007 | 2023-06-03 15:55 | alice | CRITICAL | OPEN
1004 | 2023-06-02 09:45 | alice | HIGH | OPEN
1006 | 2023-06-03 14:20 | charlie | MEDIUM | PENDING
1002 | 2023-06-01 11:05 | bob | LOW | OPEN
]]></expected_unresolved_report>

    <log_required_messages>
        Loaded CSV
        Wrote daily summary to /home/user/helpdesk/daily_summary.json
        Wrote unresolved report to /home/user/helpdesk/unresolved_report.txt
    </log_required_messages>
